title: Mean and standard deviation of sample proportions
description: Practice calculating the mean and standard deviation for the sampling distribution of a sample proportion.
topic_path: Mean and standard deviation of sample proportions

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that $29\%$ of undergraduates at a large university are involved in a campus organization. The administration plans to take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $100$ undergraduates from the population of over $20{,}000$ undergraduate students at the school to see what proportion of students sampled are involved in a campus organization.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of undergraduate students who are involved in a campus organization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $29\%$ so $p=0.29$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.29$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are more than $20{,}000$ undergraduates in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $100$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.29\left(0.71\right)}{100}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that $93\%$ of approximately $1000$ total dogs sold by a shelter are up-to-date on their shots. Administration plans to take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $40$ dogs sold by the shelter to see what proportion of the dogs sampled are up-to-date on their shots.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of dogs who are up-to-date on their shots?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $93\%$ so $p=0.93$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.93$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are approximately $1000$ dogs in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $40$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.93\left(0.07\right)}{40}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
An organization is considering having a "Bring your pet to work" day. The administration takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $35$ of their employees and finds that $20\%$ of those sampled are allergic to pets. Administration is considering taking more samples like this. Suppose that of all $500$ employees of the organization, it's actually $10\%$ that are allergic.

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $35$ employees that are allergic to pets.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $10\%$ so $p=0.1$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.1$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are over $500$ employees in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $35$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.1\left(1-0.1\right)}{35}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
The safety officers of a mining company take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $500$ employees and finds that $15\%$ of the sampled employees wear contact lenses. The safety officers may take several more samples like this. Suppose it is really $12\%$ of the approximately $34{,}000$ employees in the company who wear contact lenses.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of employees who wear contact lenses?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $12\%$ so $p=0.12$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.12$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are approximately $34{,}000$ employees in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $500$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.12\left(1-0.12\right)}{500}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that $42\%$ of students of a high school play video games at least once a month. The computer programming club takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $30$ students from the population of $792$ students at the school and finds that $40\%$ of students sampled play video games at least once a month. The club plans to take more samples like this.

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $30$ students who play video games at least once a month.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $42\%$ so $p=0.42$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.42$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are $792$ students in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $30$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.42\left(0.58\right)}{30}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
A library association plans to take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $150$ adults from the population of adults in the US to see what proportion of adults sampled believe that public libraries help them learn new things. Suppose that $76\%$ of adults in the US believe that public libraries help them learn new things.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of US adults who believe that public libraries help them learn new things?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $76\%$ so $p=0.76$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.76$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since the number of adults in the US is much more than $10$ times the sample size of $150$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.76\left(0.24\right)}{150}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
A group of science students plans to take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $25$ days in the year to see what proportion of days sampled have a low below $0^{\circ}\text{C}$ in a particular city. Suppose that $41\%$ of days in the city have a low temperature below $0^\circ\text{C}$.

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $25$ days with a low temperature below $0^\circ\text{C}$.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $41\%$ so $p=0.41$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.41$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are $365$ days in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $25$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.41\left(1-0.41\right)}{25}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
A politician takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $75$ citizens in a county to see what proportion of citizens sampled are satisfied with their standard of living. Suppose that $80\%$ of the $118{,}000$ citizens in the county are satisfied with their standard of living.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of citizens who are satisfied with their standard of living?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $80\%$ so $p=0.8$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.8$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are $118{,}000$ citizens in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $75$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.8\left(1-0.8\right)}{75}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that $65\%$ of the $310{,}000$ citizens in a county are registered to vote. A not-for-profit organization takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $100$ of these citizens and finds that $67\%$ of those sampled are registered to vote. The organization plans on taking repeated samples like this.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of citizens who are registered to vote?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $65\%$ so $p=0.65$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.65$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are $310{,}000$ citizens in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $100$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.65\left(0.35\right)}{100}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
A growth-mindset researcher plans to take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $200$ teenagers from the population of  teenagers in North America to see what proportion of teenagers sampled are pursuing a goal they have set for themselves. Suppose that $75\%$ of teenagers in North America are pursuing a goal they have set for themselves. 

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $200$ teenagers in North America who are pursuing a goal they have set for themselves.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $75\%$ so $p=0.75$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.75$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are many more  teenagers in the population than $10$ times the sample size of $200$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.75\left(0.25\right)}{200}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that $35\%$ of students in a school can sing their national anthem correctly. The music teacher takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $30$ students from the population of $750$ students at the school and finds that $40\%$ of students sampled can sing their national anthem correctly. The teacher plans to take more samples like this.

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $30$ students in the school who can sing their national anthem correctly.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $35\%$ so $p=0.35$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.35$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are $750$ students in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $30$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.35\left(1-0.35\right)}{30}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose that $3\%$ of over $200{,}000$ books borrowed from a library in a year are downloaded. The librarians plan to take an [[☃ definition 1]] of $75$ books from the population of borrowed books to see what proportion of books sampled are downloaded.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the proportion of downloaded books?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $3\%$ so $p=0.03$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.03$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are over $200{,}000$ books in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $75$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.03\left(1-0.03\right)}{75}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cable company takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $250$ of the approximately $15{,}000$ households who subscribe to them. They found that $75\%$ of the sampled households watch sports on television at least once a month. The company is considering taking more samples like this. Suppose that it is actually $70\%$ of their total subscribed households who watch those sports.

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $250$ households that watch sports on television at least once a month.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $70\%$ so $p=0.7$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.7$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are approximately $15{,}000$ households in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $250$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.7\left(0.3\right)}{250}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.

assessment_items.question_content:
The manager of a customer support center takes an [[☃ definition 1]] of $40$ reported issues and finds that $22\%$ of the sampled issues required more than one call to resolve. The manager may take an SRS like this each month. Suppose that it is really an average of $25\%$ of the approximately $1000$ issues reported per month that require more than one call.

Let $\hat p$ represent the proportion of a sample of $40$ reported issues that require more than one call to resolve.

**What are the mean and standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat p$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###The mean of the sampling distribution:

The mean of the sampling distribution of a sample proportion is equal to the population proportion. 

$\mu_{\hat{p}}=p$

The population proportion reported is $25\%$ so $p=0.25$.

$\mu_{\hat p}=p=0.25$

###The standard deviation of the sampling distribution:

Since there are approximately $1000$ issues in the population, which is more than $10$ times the sample size of $40$, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution of $\hat{p}$ can be found by using the formula below.

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{p\left(1-p\right)}{n}}$

So, the standard deviation of the sampling distribution is

$\sigma_{\hat{p}}=\sqrt{\dfrac{0.25\left(0.75\right)}{40}}$

options.definition: A simple random sample (SRS) is a sample taken so that each member and set of $n$ members has an equal chance of being in the sample.
